title: Jordanian Mansaf
description: A classic Jordanian dish made with tender lamb cooked in a rich yogurt sauce and served over rice.

ingredients.name: Beef (or lamb), cut into small pieces
ingredients.notes: You can also use lamb if preferred

ingredients.name: Onion, chopped
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount based on your preference

ingredients.name: Mansaf spice blend
ingredients.notes: A mix of cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and black pepper

ingredients.name: Yogurt
ingredients.notes: Full-fat yogurt works best for this dish

ingredients.name: Rice
ingredients.notes: White or brown rice can be used

ingredients.name: Delicate spices (such as cardamom and rosewater)

instructions: Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the mansaf spice blend and stir to coat the lamb.
Add the yogurt and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let the mixture cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the rice according to package instructions.
To serve, place a scoop of rice on a plate, then top with the lamb and sauce.
Garnish with delicate spices, if desired.
